key_insight: The paper shows that contact-induced facial wrinkles can be realistically simulated by combining viscoelastic skin behavior with anatomically informed ligament constraints in a finite element model.
engineering_relevance: This provides a physics-based tool for generating temporally coherent facial deformation in animation, virtual humans, and biomechanical analysis.
method: Model skin as a viscoelastic layered material with time-dependent relaxation.
Use high-order prismatic solid-shell finite elements to capture through-thickness stress and fine wrinkles.
Add continuum-based skin ligaments to represent heterogeneous attachments and mobility constraints.
evidence_signal: The method is evaluated on synthetic examples and compared against real-world footage, showing visually realistic transient wrinkle patterns.
watch_item: Accuracy may depend on careful calibration of viscoelastic parameters, ligament attachments, and mesh resolution to preserve small-scale wrinkling.
paper_title: Physics-Based Simulation of Contact-Induced Facial Wrinkling
